across.hint: This modal is used to give advice or a recommendation, e.g. You ___ eat more vegetables.
This modal expresses present ability or possibility, e.g. I ___ swim.
The negative form expressing inability or prohibition, e.g. You ___ park here.
Used for polite requests or offers, e.g. ___ you like some tea?
Expresses future actions or decisions made at the moment of speaking.
A formal modal used to ask for or grant permission, e.g. ___ I come in?
Expresses a weak or uncertain possibility, e.g. It ___ rain today.

down.hint: Expresses strong obligation or necessity, e.g. You ___ finish your homework.
Used for formal suggestions or offers, especially in first person, e.g. ___ we begin?
The past tense of can, used to express past ability, e.g. I ___ ride a bike when I was five.
